headline: South Korea fight back to beat Czechia 2-1 on World Cup 2026 opening day
summary: In Group A at Estadio Akron, Guadalajara on June 11, 2026, South Korea came from behind to beat Czechia 2-1. Czechia led through a Ladislav Krejčí header (59') from a Vladimír Coufal long throw. South Korea responded fast: Hwang In-beom equalised (67') with a clever cut and chipped finish, set up by a Lee Kang-in through ball, then substitute Oh Hyeon-gyu — on for captain Son Heung-min — tapped in the winner (80') from a Hwang In-beom cross. Czechia thought they had restored parity when Tomáš Souček headed in from a free kick (77') but it was correctly ruled out for offside. South Korea dominated possession (55%) and chances; Son created openings but couldn't score, and the Czech keeper made a notable 49' double save before Korea's comeback.
